items.claim: McMillan maintains a high-intensity pace throughout the entire fight, even when fatigued.
items.quote: the way that McMillan fights, it's just like even if he's tired, he's just going 100%, and that is really tough for people to keep up with.

items.claim: McMillan possesses high-level takedown defense and a strong sprawl.
items.quote: Good ground and pound, good takedown defense, got a good sprawl

items.claim: McMillan is physically superior to Montes.
items.quote: he's also just a little bit better in the physicality and the areas there.

items.claim: Christian Leroy Duncan is an unorthodox striker who leaves himself open for counters.
items.quote: very unorthodox style, which is great, but it does leave him open for counters sometimes.

items.claim: Jared Cannonier holds the record for most strikes landed in a single fight in the middleweight division.
items.quote: I believe he even holds the record for strikes landed in this division.

items.claim: Jared Cannonier has high-level submission defense and is difficult to hold on the ground.
items.quote: He's got good submission defense. Um he's really hard to hold down.

items.claim: Kamaru Usman develops a technical flaw where he drops his jab hand instead of returning it to his face as the fight progresses.
items.quote: he'll start dropping the jab down instead of bringing it back to his face after he throws it.

items.claim: Kamaru Usman clears his opponent's legs during takedowns to secure side control or half guard rather than landing in full guard.
items.quote: Instead of getting the takedown and end up in his in his opponent's guard, he'll often clear the legs to get to the side and end up either in half guard or side control.

items.claim: Dricus Du Plessis fights at a chaotic pace that disrupts the ability of opponents to execute their game plans.
items.quote: The pace that he fights at is just so chaotic and uh it it makes guys even with good cardio kind of not feel like they've got the ability to to put things together
